content.problemStatement:
There are NNN employees (numbered 111 through NNN) working at ShareChat. You are given MMM pairs of employees that do not get along with each other.

Arya, the CEO of ShareChat, wants to throw a party. She is still not sure which employees to invite, but her assistant has given her QQQ suggestions. Each suggestion consists of KKK intervals [L1,R1],[L2,R2],‚Ä¶,[LK,RK][L_1, R_1], [L_2, R_2], \ldots, [L_K, R_K][L1‚Äã,R1‚Äã],[L2‚Äã,R2‚Äã],‚Ä¶,[LK‚Äã,RK‚Äã]; this means that all employees whose numbers lie in the union of these intervals should be invited to the party.

Arya is a perfectionist, so she wants all invited employees to get along. You, her second assistant, are the only one she could trust with finding out, for each suggestion, if this condition is satisfied. Complete this task!
constraints: 1‚â§Li‚â§Ri‚â§N1 \le L_i \le R_i \le N1‚â§Li‚Äã‚â§Ri‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
the sum of KKK over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
Each of the following MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv indicating that employees uuu and vvv do not get along.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ.
The following QQQ lines describe suggestions. Each of these lines contains an integer KKK, followed by a space and 2K2K2K space-separated integers L1,R1,L2,R2,‚Ä¶,LK,RKL_1, R_1, L_2, R_2, \ldots, L_K, R_KL1‚Äã,R1‚Äã,L2‚Äã,R2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,LK‚Äã,RK‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each suggestion, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if all invited employees get along or `"NO"` otherwise.
